steps.title: Factor out 3 from the x terms
steps.content:
The equation is $3x^2 - 6x - 30 = 0$. Notice that the first two terms both have a factor of 3: $3x^2 - 6x = 3(x^2 - 2x)$. So we can rewrite the entire equation as:
$$3(x^2 - 2x) - 30 = 0$$

steps.title: Isolate the target expression
steps.content:
Since $3(x^2 - 2x) - 30 = 0$, add 30 to both sides:
$$3(x^2 - 2x) = 30$$
Divide by 3:
$$x^2 - 2x = 10$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
